text: Language, identity and power are the key concepts explored in this chapter. The author examines language use as a performance in social life and discusses how individuals can exercise power and gain status in a highly complex and potentially fragmented social world. The study draws on a wide range of theoretical perspectives from across the disciplines. A critical exploration of the ways in which discourse relates to changing patterns of globalisation, this work advances our understanding of the complex and dynamic relationship between communication and identity.
